mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that immediately suppress consumer spending expectations and trigger negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3pp in a single month
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.3pp, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below 52% in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales confirms broad-based demand growth that sustains corporate earnings trajectories, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal yields negative 60-day returns in > 55% of occurrences, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant in quarterly regression models.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp contractions in the index of industrial production during a late-cycle expansion indicate capacity constraints and supply bottlenecks that immediately pressure equity valuations, causing negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in > 60% of late-cycle periods following INDPRO declines, or the mechanism fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark during late-cycle regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Core inflation exceeding 2.5% forces expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, which raises discount rates and compresses equity multiples, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in > 60% of periods where CPIAUCSL YoY exceeds 2.5%, or the signal Sharpe ratio remains below 0.1 over a 5-year evaluation window.

mechanism_narrative: Steady increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect expanding economic activity and rising wage income, which directly supports corporate revenue growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.01 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no significant positive correlation with monthly payroll growth.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in housing starts during a post-shock recovery phase signals weakening residential investment that drags on broader economic momentum, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in > 65% of post-shock recovery periods following HOUST declines, or the signal predictive power vanishes when controlling for interest rate changes.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising industrial production and falling inflation indicates improving supply-side efficiency and margin expansion, which immediately boosts equity valuations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The divergence signal yields negative 5-day returns in > 55% of occurrences, or the interaction effect becomes statistically insignificant in high-frequency regression tests.
